name: Spain
description: Spain is a large western European country spanning Atlantic and Mediterranean coasts and a dry interior, a major destination for remote workers and retirees. The figures below are screened, sourced, and dated. They are not advice.

priceLevelIndexEU27.sourceName: Eurostat, price level index for actual individual consumption (tec00120, EU27=100)
priceLevelIndexEU27.excerpt: Spain price level index for actual individual consumption (EU27 = 100): 91.6 in 2025 (100 = EU average).

averageJanuaryHighC.sourceName: Madrid climate normals (AEMET 1991-2020, Retiro), via Wikipedia
averageJanuaryHighC.excerpt: Madrid, the capital, shown as representative for Spain: mean daily maximum 10.0 C in January (1991 to 2020 normals).

averageJulyHighC.sourceName: Madrid climate normals (AEMET 1991-2020, Retiro), via Wikipedia
averageJulyHighC.excerpt: Madrid: mean daily maximum 32.8 C in July (1991 to 2020 normals).

averageAnnualSunHours.excerpt: Madrid: about 2,744 mean annual sunshine hours.

koppenClass.sourceName: GloH2O Koppen-Geiger climate classification (Beck et al. 2023)
koppenClass.excerpt: Koppen-Geiger class Csa, hot-summer Mediterranean, across central Spain; coastal and northern areas differ.

digitalNomadVisa.excerpt: Spain telework (digital nomad) visa: minimum income of 200 percent of the SMI; the 2026 SMI is 17,094 euros per year (Real Decreto 126/2026), so about 2,849 euros per month.

goldenVisa.value: Abolished on 3 April 2025 under Organic Law 1/2025. Investor residence permits tied to real estate investment over 500,000 euros no longer exist.
goldenVisa.sourceName: La Moncloa (Government of Spain press office), end of the golden visa
goldenVisa.excerpt: The government ends, on 3 April 2025, residence permits linked to real estate investment over 500,000 euros, known as the golden visa.

headlinePersonalIncomeTaxRate.sourceName: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries, Spain, taxes on personal income
headlinePersonalIncomeTaxRate.excerpt: State top rate 47 percent on income above 300,000 euros; combined state and regional top rates typically range from 47 to 54 percent by autonomous community.

specialRegime.value: Beckham regime (Article 93 LIRPF): 24 percent flat on Spanish employment income up to 600,000 euros (47 percent above), for the arrival year plus five years.
specialRegime.sourceName: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries, Spain, other issues
specialRegime.excerpt: 24 percent for the first 600,000 euros of taxable income and 47 percent on any excess; the regime applies in the year of the option and the following five years.

physiciansPer1000.sourceName: WHO Global Health Observatory, medical doctors per 10,000, via Wikipedia
physiciansPer1000.excerpt: Spain: 44.8 medical doctors per 10,000 (about 4.5 per 1,000), WHO 2021. Counts doctors licensed to practise, which overstates practising doctors.

peaceIndexScore.sourceName: Institute for Economics and Peace, Global Peace Index 2024
peaceIndexScore.excerpt: Spain 2024 Global Peace Index score 1.597 (rank 23 of 163). Lower is more peaceful.
